summary_paragraph: On September 14, 2009, the Supreme Court Professional Conduct Committee (PCC) filed a petition recommending that the respondent, Lynn D. Morse, be suspended from the practice of law for two years. We order that the respondent be disbarred.

case_name: Michelle Hemenway v. Edmund J. Hemenway, Jr.
author: per_curiam
summary_paragraph: The defendant, Edmund J. Hemenway, Jr., appeals a final order of protection issued by the Derry Family Division (Moore, J.), arguing that the trial court lacked subject matter and personal jurisdiction. We affirm in part and reverse in part and remand.

case_name: In the Matter of Dawn M. O'Neil and Eugene E. O'Neil, Jr.
lower_court: family court and at his workplace; (2)
author: per_curiam
summary_paragraph: The respondent, Eugene E. O’Neil, Jr., challenges numerous rulings entered by the Concord Family Division in divorce proceedings with the petitioner, Dawn M. O’Neil, including: (1) restraining orders restricting his presence at the family court and at his workplace; (2

case_name: State of New Hampshire v. Reno Demesmin
author: hicks
summary_paragraph: The defendant, Reno Demesmin, appeals his conviction of first degree assault. See RSA 631:1 (2007). He argues that the Superior Court (Smukler, J.) erred when it permitted the re-evaluation of his competency to stand trial. We affirm.

case_name: Georgia Tuttle, M.D. & a. v. New Hampshire Medical Malpractice Joint Underwriting Association & a.
author: per_curiam

author: per_curiam

case_name: Megan Smith v. City of Franklin
author: per_curiam

hicks.note: BRODERICK, C.J., and DALIANIS, DUGGAN and HICKS, JJ., concurred.

summary_paragraph: The plaintiff, Megan Smith, appeals an order of the Superior Court (Mangones, J.) upholding the denial of her request for financial assistance by the defendant, City of Franklin (City). The trial court ruled that the City correctly interpreted RSA 167:27 (2002) to preclude the plaintiff’s

case_name: State of New Hampshire v. Christopher Horak
author: hicks
summary_paragraph: The defendant, Christopher Horak, appeals his conviction of sexual assault for engaging in sexual contact with a person thirteen years of age or older whom he knew to be mentally defective. See RSA 632-A:4, I(a), :2, I(h) (2007). We reverse and remand.
